era: 1995-present. Program Director / on-air host. E=3 -- quiet curatorial authority. KEXP 90.3 FM. The programming architect who built KEXP's identity. Also performs as Tablam.

location.formative: KEXP 90.3 FM (formerly KCMU). Capitol Hill. The new KEXP building at Seattle Center (opened 2016). Programming office and on-air studio.
location.career: KEXP Program Director (~1995-present). On-air host. Built KEXP's programming philosophy, talent development, live session platform, and global streaming identity. Tablam musical project.

members.name: Kevin Cole
members.note: KEXP's programming architect since the KCMU era. Built the curatorial framework that defines the station's identity. Mentored on-air talent (130-132). Championed live sessions. Also performs as Tablam.

associated_musicians.name: Cheryl Waters
associated_musicians.note: The Midday Show. Local/indie focus. E=3. Operates within Cole's programming framework.

associated_musicians.name: John Richards
associated_musicians.note: The Morning Show. Personal, vulnerable. E=3. Music Heals. Mentored within Cole's station structure.

associated_musicians.name: Darek Mazzone
associated_musicians.note: Wo' Pop. World music. E=4. Cole built the programming structure that enables Wo' Pop's global scope.

associated_musicians.name: Bruce Pavitt
associated_musicians.note: Sub Pop co-founder. E=3 curation (indie/alternative). Cole applies the same curatorial DNA to radio programming.

associated_musicians.name: Nasty Nes Rodriguez
associated_musicians.note: KCMU hip-hop DJ, E=4. Helped build the freeform tradition Cole inherited and professionalized.

theory_nodes.concept: Programming architect -- the person who built the stage the performers work on; post-trio infrastructure; Cole shaped KEXP's curatorial philosophy, hired and mentored talent, built the live session platform, guided KCMU-to-KEXP transition
examples: Host trio (130-132) are the performers. Cole (133) is the architect who built their stage.
Live in-studio sessions: hundreds of millions of YouTube views. A programming innovation Cole championed.
KCMU-to-KEXP transition: maintaining freeform spirit while professionalizing the reach.
New building at Seattle Center (2016): radio as community institution, not just broadcast technology.
theory_nodes.description: Programming architect. The infrastructure behind the on-air voices. Post-trio documentation.

ancestors: KCMU freeform tradition (126-128). The institutional foundation Cole inherited.
KEXP host trio (130-132). The on-air talent Cole mentored and empowered.
genealogy.relationship: Architect at degree 133. The invisible structure that enables the visible performances.

theory_nodes.concept: KEXP curatorial model -- human curation over algorithm; discovery over repetition; depth over breadth; live sessions as global export; local-to-global artist pipeline; listener-supported sustainability
examples: Human curation as competitive advantage against Spotify, YouTube, Apple Music algorithms.
Live sessions: intimate, high-fidelity, distributed globally. The most effective artist discovery mechanism in indie radio.
DJ freedom within station coherence. Too much freedom = chaos. Too much structure = format radio. Cole found the middle.
Listener-supported funding. Quality curation drives the support that sustains the model.
theory_nodes.description: KEXP curatorial model. Human curation over algorithm. Cole's architectural legacy.

ancestors: KCMU freeform tradition. The philosophical foundation.
Paul Allen donation enabling the KEXP transition. The financial foundation.
genealogy.relationship: Curatorial model at degree 133. The framework that makes KEXP work.

theory_nodes.concept: Tablam -- the program director as artist; atmospheric, experimental musical project; curator-to-creator duality; strict separation between curatorial role and artistic output; ethics of non-self-promotion
examples: Tablam: atmospheric, electronic, ambient. Post-rock and ambient electronica.
Cole does not use KEXP to promote Tablam. Strict ethical separation.
The artistic output reflects the same values he curates for: attention-rewarding, compositionally interesting.
Tablam's low profile demonstrates the program director's ethics.
theory_nodes.description: Tablam. Curator as creator. The program director's artistic dimension.

ancestors: Cole's decades of curation creating deep musical knowledge.
The KEXP ethos of respecting music as art, not product.
genealogy.relationship: Tablam at degree 133. The artist within the architect.

theory_nodes.concept: E=3 quiet curatorial authority -- infrastructure energy; the architect doesn't need to be the loudest voice; matches KEXP's E=3 baseline (Waters 130, Richards 131); effortless confidence from decades of curation
examples: E=3 matches Waters (130) and Richards (131). The KEXP curatorial baseline.
Lower than Mazzone's E=4 (132). Infrastructure doesn't need performative energy.
On-air voice: warm, knowledgeable, understated. Effortless authority.
Tablam's aesthetic is also E=3: atmospheric, patient, textured.
theory_nodes.description: E=3 quiet curatorial authority. Infrastructure energy. The KEXP baseline.

ancestors: E=3 established at Waters (130) and Richards (131) for KEXP's quiet baseline.
E=3 at Pavitt (128) for curatorial infrastructure energy.
genealogy.relationship: E=3 architect energy at degree 133. The person who defined the baseline.

cross_references.relationship: KEXP TALENT! Waters (Midday, 130, local, E=3) operates within Cole's programming framework. Cole mentored and empowered the host trio. Shared E=3 curatorial baseline.
KEXP TALENT! Richards (Morning, 131, personal, E=3) operates within Cole's framework. The personal voice enabled by the architect's structure.
KEXP TALENT! Mazzone (Wo' Pop, 132, global, E=4) exists because Cole built a station that enables global music programming. The architect's framework at work.
CURATORIAL LINEAGE! Pavitt (Sub Pop, 128, E=3) curated a label. Cole (KEXP, 133, E=3) curates a station. Same curatorial DNA, different institutional forms.
KCMU LINEAGE! Nasty Nes (KCMU, 126, E=4) helped build the freeform tradition Cole inherited and professionalized as KEXP Program Director.
